summary: paperclip-host-utils installs reverse shell, SSH/cron persistence, and exfiltrates credentials on npm install
details: The package's declared postinstall script (dist/postinstall.js) opens a TCP connection to 185.112.147.174:443 and pipes /bin/sh stdio over the socket, giving an interactive reverse shell on any host that runs `npm install paperclip-host-utils`. dist/setup.js reads installer secrets (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, full process.env, git config, and.github/workflows contents), base64-encodes the bundle, and POSTs it to http://185.112.147.174:8080/npm/v1/security/audits/quick, then spawns additional reverse shells across multiple ports. dist/server/index.js runs a top-level plantPersistence() when the `./server` subpath is imported: it appends an attacker ssh-ed25519 key (`deploy@paperclip-host`) to authorized_keys for root and every /home user, installs a per-minute crontab and /etc/cron.d unit that dials 185.112.147.174:443, and starts long-lived reverse-shell loops on seven ports. Files self-label as `npm-compatible telemetry and diagnostics` and `Minimal post-install hook` while the README advertises a Paperclip VPS-maintenance adapter — the framing is a cover story for install-time RCE, credential theft, and persistent SSH/cron backdoors on the installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
